compiled_state.title: Tier C: compute-heavy read-side bash -> rebar._reads behind REBAR_COMPUTE
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Tier C of the bash->Python strangler-fig (story adult-oxide-slave, docs/bash-migration.md §5). Tier B retired the leaf writes; Tier C ports the compute-heavy read-side bash that orchestrates the reducer/graph with formatting on top. These are read-only over replayed state, so no write-path risk — the parity surface is ORDERING and FORMATTING determinism.

## What
Port behind REBAR_COMPUTE (kill-switch already in rebar._switch): next-batch (ticket-next-batch.sh ~954 LOC, conflict-aware file-impact scheduling), validate (validate-issues.sh ~945, repo-wide health + finding buckets), list-epics (ticket-list-epics.sh ~453). Riders at the same shape: list-descendants, summary, clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check. Reuse the Tier A read plumbing (in-process replay, freshness, --no-sync) + rebar.reducer/rebar.graph.

## Scope
Read-side compute only. Known parity traps (pin with golden matrices): bash sort vs Python sorted (locale/stability), jq number/escape formatting vs json.dumps, validate finding bucketing/severity order, next-batch batch composition over crafted file-impact overlaps (agents' parallel dispatch depends on this determinism). Each command spawns a child; its bash suite is the characterization gate, dual-run byte-identical before the default flips.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] REBAR_COMPUTE wired at dispatcher + library/MCP; default bash until parity+soak
- [ ] next-batch, validate, list-epics ported to rebar._reads/_commands, reusing reducer/graph
- [ ] Golden ordering/formatting matrices pin the parity traps (sort/locale, jq vs json.dumps, validate buckets, next-batch composition)
- [ ] Each command's bash suite dual-run green (bash AND python, byte-identical)
- [ ] Default stays bash until flip+soak; the three big scripts (~2350 LOC, 12 heredocs) retire after

comments.body:
Tier C progress (2026-06-12):
- list-epics: retired via deprecation (worm-burr-fly) — done.
- next-batch: PORTED behind REBAR_COMPUTE (sure-beech-taunt, closed). In-process via rebar._engine_support.next_batch(+next_batch_files); dispatcher _compute_python helper pinned to rebar._switch; dual-run byte-identical; library/MCP now in-process (last subprocessing read gone). Surfaced+fixed a latent bash conflict_file nondeterminism (sorted iteration). Committed 92caa823.
- validate: remaining real port — gawk-grove-site (open), AC + file-impact set, linked discovered_from.
Default still bash for next-batch pending the release flip+soak; bash scripts retire at tier end.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Tier C PORTING COMPLETE (2026-06-12).
- list-epics: deprecated (worm-burr-fly) — done.
- next-batch: ported behind REBAR_COMPUTE (sure-beech-taunt, closed) — commit 92caa823.
- validate: ported behind REBAR_COMPUTE (gawk-grove-site, closed) — commit 6f3ced04.

Both real ports are in-process (reuse reducer/graph + shared list_states), dual-run byte-identical/semantic-equal across REBAR_COMPUTE=bash|python, gated by test_next_batch_compute.py (23) + test_validate_compute.py (20). Interfaces tier 288/288. Default still bash.

Remaining for the tier (NOT porting — release-gated):
1. Flip REBAR_COMPUTE default bash->python (one commit).
2. Soak (rest of release cycle): fsck clean, no PUSH_PENDING anomalies, no exit-code regressions, perf gates green.
3. Retire: delete ticket-next-batch.sh (954) + validate-issues.sh (945) + their heredocs + REBAR_COMPUTE + _compute_python; translate the bash suites to pytest; architecture.md offender table loses both compute entries.
Keeping this story in_progress until retirement.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
REBAR_COMPUTE default FLIPPED bash->python (2026-06-12, commit 0fae13f4) — Tier C step 5 done.
- _switch default + dispatcher _compute_python (expansion+comparison) + parity test default-case moved together (ce2dfcf5 pattern). Rollback lever REBAR_COMPUTE=bash retained.
- Evidence: 43 Tier-C parity cases + both bash suites green under unset default; interfaces tier 288/288; fsck clean.

Now in SOAK (step 6): default python exercised by this repo's own dogfooding for the rest of the release cycle. Exit criteria: fsck clean, no PUSH_PENDING anomalies, no exit-code regressions in CI/live use, perf gates green. After soak: retire ticket-next-batch.sh + validate-issues.sh + REBAR_COMPUTE + _compute_python, translate suites (architecture.md offender table loses both compute entries).

NOTE: the on-PATH pipx rebar is still 0.5.0 (pre-flip). To dogfood the flipped working tree, 'pipx install --editable . --force' (revert: 'pipx install nava-rebar --force'). Keeping this story in_progress through soak+retire.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Dogfood-soak validation (2026-06-12): switched the environment to the editable working-tree build (pipx install --editable .) — default REBAR_COMPUTE=python now live on PATH. Exercised the full ticket surface:
- probe-rebar.sh ISOLATED: 141/141 passed (every command via the live engine).
- probe-rebar.sh PROBE_LIVE=1 (real store): 141/141 passed; store verified unchanged (17 open/in_progress before and after).
- Tier C live cross-checks vs forced REBAR_COMPUTE=bash: validate semantic-equal + byte-identical terse stderr + exit 4 both; next-batch batch composition equal on nervy-hold-dip + the migration epic.
- MCP/library reads (validate, next_batch, list, search, ready, show) all in-process under python default, schema-valid.
No regression observed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
TIER C COMPLETE & RETIRED (2026-06-12).
Full §2 lifecycle done for all three compute commands:
- list-epics: deprecated (worm-burr-fly).
- next-batch: ported (sure-beech-taunt) -> flipped -> soaked -> retired.
- validate: ported (gawk-grove-site) -> flipped -> soaked -> retired (785b-7c9f).
Retirement commit 3567a2ff deleted both bash scripts + REBAR_COMPUTE + _compute_python + dual-impl wiring (~1,899 LOC). Python is the sole impl. Closing this story — Tier C is done. Next: Tier D (write/sync core, the crux), which folds in bug stiff-mop-lane.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
